title: Senior Full Stack Software & Data/AI Engineer
company_focus: Global financial services and insurance company
description:
Description and Requirements The Team You Will Join MetLife
is on a transformational journey into the New Frontier.
Working closely with our business partners, we are embarking
on a transformation that will drive a holistic and cohesive
product-aligned model, enabled by an end-to-end delivery
capability to drive our highest impact enterprise strategic
agenda. Within MetLife Global Technology - Corporate Systems
- Controls, our team assists our IT and business partners in
solving complex operational and risk management challenges
by rapidly delivering lasting transformations with speed and
discipline. Driven by passion and purpose, you will be
instrumental in driving change and implementing MetLife's
product model for our businesses while aligning our brand
and technology to bring immediate value to our customers and
partners. The Opportunity MetLife Finance & Risk Management
Technology - Controls IT is seeking a Senior Software
Development Engineer. The ideal candidate will possess
strong development and engineering capabilities,
communication, collaboration, with hands-on experience in
custom software development and data engineering, supporting
Risk, Compliance, and Internal Audit functions. Solid
understanding of best practices and developer enablement
required. The role is both hands-on and strategic, often
guiding teams through architectural decisions, platform
modernization, and agile delivery practices. How You'll Help
Us Build a Confident Future (Key Responsibilities)

- Partner with product owners to plan, prioritize, and deliver
  high‑impact business outcomes.
- Lead the design, development, and testing of custom software
  components and data processing pipelines, including the
  creation and maintenance of architectural documentation.
- Oversee operational support activities and conduct
  root‑cause analysis for complex systems and workflows.
- Champion and promote best practices across AI/ML, DevOps,
  Agile methodologies, and Cloud technologies.

 Required Skills:

- Bachelor's degree in computer science, Information Systems,
  Engineering, or a related field.
- 7+ years of hands-on experience in custom software
  development (React, Java, and Python) within a cloud-based
  DevOps environment, including strong familiarity with
  CI/CD automation.
- 3+ years of practical experience with most of the following
  AI/ML technologies and libraries (examples include:
  PySpark, Pandas, NumPy, LangChain, Hugging Face, LLM/RAG
  integration, prompt engineering).
- 3+ years of hands-on experience building and optimizing Data
  Engineering pipelines using technologies such as
  Databricks, Hadoop, PySpark, Delta Tables, and/or Airflow.
- Strong proficiency in database design, software design
  patterns, performance tuning, and API integration.
- Hands‑on experience with Agile frameworks and DevOps
  pipelines.
- Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the
  ability to build strong partnerships across technical and
  business stakeholders.
- Strong analytical, problem‑solving, process‑improvement, and
  technical‑leadership capabilities.
- Proven ability to translate strategic business objectives
  into actionable technical deliverables, including user
  story creation and backlog management.
- Experience working in complex global organizations,
  collaborating effectively across multiple business units
  and regions.

 Preferred Skills:

- Familiarity with Enterprise Risk Management, Compliance,
  Internal Audit, or other GRC-related domains.
- Experience working with Azure DevOps.
- Experience with infrastructure automation (Terraform).
- Master's degree preferred.
- Experience working within Financial Services or a global
  insurance organization.


Location Expectation: This is a hybrid role requiring a
minimum of 3 days per week in office.  The expected salary
range for this position is $103,300 - $154,975. This role
may also be eligible for annual short-term incentive
compensation. All incentives and benefits are subject to the
applicable plan terms. Benefits We Offer Our U.S. benefits
address holistic well-being with programs for physical and
mental health, financial wellness, and support for families.
We offer a comprehensive health plan that includes
medical/prescription drug and vision, dental insurance, and
no-cost short- and long-term disability. We also provide
company-paid life insurance and legal services, a retirement
pension funded entirely by MetLife and 401(k) with employer
matching, group discounts on voluntary insurance products
including auto and home, pet, critical illness, hospital
indemnity, and accident insurance, as well as Employee
Assistance Program (EAP) and digital mental health programs,
parental leave, volunteer time off, tuition assistance and
much more! About MetLife Recognized on Fortune magazine's
list of the "World's Most Admired Companies", Fortune
World's 25 Best Workplaces™, as well as the Fortune 100 Best
Companies to Work For®, MetLife, through its subsidiaries
and affiliates, is one of the world's leading financial
services companies; providing insurance, annuities, employee
benefits and asset management to individual and
institutional customers. With operations in more than 40
markets, we hold leading positions in the United States,
Latin America, Asia, Europe, and the Middle East. Our
purpose is simple - to help our colleagues, customers,
communities, and the world at large create a more confident
future. United by purpose and guided by our core values -
Win Together, Do the Right Thing, Deliver Impact Over
Activity, and Think Ahead - we're inspired to transform the
next century in financial services. At MetLife, it's
#AllTogetherPossible . Join us! MetLife is an Equal
Opportunity Employer. All employment decisions are made
without regards to race, color, national origin, religion,
creed, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related
medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender identity or
expression, age, disability, marital or domestic/civil
partnership status, genetic information, citizenship status
(although applicants and employees must be legally
authorized to work in the United States), uniformed service
member or veteran status, or any other characteristic
protected by applicable federal, state, or local law
("protected characteristics"). If you need an accommodation
due to a disability, please email us at
accommodations@metlife.com. This information will be held in
confidence and used only to determine an appropriate
accommodation for the application process. MetLife maintains
a drug-free workplace. $103,300 - $154,975 #BI-Hybrid
responsibilities: Partner with product owners to plan, prioritize, and deliver high-impact business outcomes
Lead the design, development, and testing of custom software components and data processing pipelines
Create and maintain architectural documentation for software systems
Oversee operational support activities and conduct root-cause analysis for complex systems and workflows
Champion and promote best practices across AI/ML, DevOps, Agile methodologies, and Cloud technologies
Guide teams through architectural decisions, platform modernization, and agile delivery practices
Translate strategic business objectives into actionable technical deliverables including user stories and backlog management
use_cases: Enterprise GRC (Governance, Risk, and Compliance) processes
Financial services data processing and analytics
ai_classification_reasoning:
This role requires 3+ years of hands-on experience with
AI/ML technologies including LangChain, Hugging Face,
LLM/RAG integration, and prompt engineering. The candidate
will build and implement AI systems directly, not just
support infrastructure for AI work.
